texto: Don't you be worried or annoyed, Sancho, about any comments you hear, or there will never be an end to them. Keep a safe conscience and let people say what they like: trying to still gossips' tongues is like putting up doors in open fields. If the governor leaves office rich they say he's a thief, and if he leaves it poor they say he's a milksop and a fool.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Miguel de Cervantes Saavedra: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “Don't you be worried or annoyed, Sancho, about any comments you hear, or there will never be an e…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que sabedoria, conscience, gossip pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de trabalho e termino.

texto: In any case, Cide Hamete Benengeli was a very careful historian, and very accurate in all things, as can be clearly seen in the details he relates to us, for although they are trivial and inconsequential, he does not attempt to pass over them in silence; his example could be followed by solemn historians who recount actions so briefly and succinctly that we can barely taste them, and leave behind in the inkwell, through carelessness, malice, or ignorance, the most substantive part of the work.
explicacao: Citação de Miguel de Cervantes Saavedra sobre temas ligados a trabalho. “In any case, Cide Hamete Benengeli was a very careful historian, and very accurate in all things,…” articula trabalho, history, writing em registro de trabalho, com relevância para contextos como reflexao-noturna e trabalho.
